contaminant.name: Concrete Efflorescence
contaminant.description: Efflorescence appears as white, crystalline salt deposits that form when moisture draws soluble salts through porous materials like concrete or brick, crystallizing on the surface as water evaporates. This contamination sets it apart from dirt or algae by its powdery, water-soluble nature that flakes easily yet recurs without addressing underlying moisture issues, behaving more aggressively on absorbent stones such as limestone or sandstone where it embeds deeply. Removal poses a solid challenge due to the risk of substrate damage from abrasive methods, but laser cleaning addresses this effectively by vaporizing the salts layer by layer while preserving the material's integrity.

fireExplosionRisk.description: Minimal fire risk with standard precautions and adequate ventilation
fireExplosionRisk.mitigation: Standard fire safety precautions, extinguisher available within 15m

toxicGasRisk.description: Crystalline Silica (as respirable dust) and Carbon Dioxide generation - multiple toxic compounds
toxicGasRisk.mitigation: N95 or P100 respirator for particulate control, standard ventilation. WARNING: Crystalline Silica (as respirable dust) - known carcinogen(s), minimize exposure

visibilityHazard.description: Moderate visibility reduction (40-60%), significant particulate haze
visibilityHazard.mitigation: Ensure clear sight lines, use source extraction, maintain awareness of surroundings

ventilationRequirements.rationale: Standard industrial ventilation (10 ACH) for particulate control
